description_long: This is a tall, narrow-necked ceramic vase with a bulbous body and a single, curved handle extending from the upper neck to the shoulder. The rim is flared and undulating, giving it a distinctive, flower-like appearance. The surface is decorated with faded reddish-brown geometric motifs, including lines and triangular shapes, set against a pale, earthy background. Visible cracks and repairs suggest the vase is an ancient artifact that has been restored. The handle is robust and slightly irregular, indicating handcrafting. The overall finish is matte with a slightly rough texture, typical of historical pottery.
description: A tall, bulbous ceramic vase with a flared rim, single handle, and faded reddish geometric patterns on a pale background. The surface shows visible cracks and restoration.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flared rim, narrow neck, and geometric patterns on the body.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full handle and more of the geometric motifs, with the spout visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the handle attachment and less visible decoration, with restoration lines apparent.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the handle from a different angle and a large, pale area with minimal decoration.
